Template 2 — Wednesday + Friday Paired Product Mockup · Sample copy

1080×1350 portrait (4:5, LinkedIn-native) · One master, two variants · For sign-off only — copy is placeholder

Wednesday — Educational
Mechanism
{PRODUCT_NAME}

{HEADLINE} A short hook line, two lines max.

Standard dose{KEY_FACT}
HDDWaterwellCoringDiamond Coring
Blick.
www.blick.group
Friday — Spotlight
Spotlight
{PRODUCT_NAME}

{HEADLINE} Different hook, same product, sentence case.

Standard dose{KEY_FACT}
HDDWaterwellCoringDiamond Coring
Blick.
www.blick.group

Template 2 — Wed/Fri Paired Product

The two variants

Same master, identical layout. Only difference: kicker chip flips colour — ink-black on Wednesday (Mechanism), brand orange on Friday (Spotlight). Signals the pair to followers without changing layout. No second orange element competes.

Replaceable fields

  • {KICKER} — "Mechanism" (Wed) / "Spotlight" (Fri)
  • {PRODUCT_NAME} — uppercase, e.g. "TORQUE DOWN"
  • {HEADLINE} — the hook line, sentence case
  • {KEY_FACT} — one supporting fact, e.g. "2 L/m³"
  • {TAGS[]} — disciplines (max 4)

Hard rules

  • Kicker chip is the ONLY orange element competing with the rule. No third.
  • No product photo in this template — type-driven. Photos can be added later as a variant if asset library exists.
  • No em dashes in any copy
  • Sentence case throughout

Why portrait (1080×1350)

  • 4:5 = LinkedIn's recommended portrait aspect, no crop in feed
  • Portrait gives more vertical room for the hook to breathe — fixes the listy/cramped feel of the existing Carry Flow card
